channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Iran attacks world's largest liquified natural gas complex in Qatar
item.description: Iran struck Qatar's massive gas facility after Israel targeted Iran's gas field, putting global energy supplies at risk.
item.content:encoded: Iran struck Qatar's massive gas facility after Israel targeted Iran's gas field, putting global energy supplies at risk.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: What did President Trump know about Iran before going to war
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Nate Swanson, former director for Iran at the National Security Council, about what President Trump understood about Iran before going to war.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Nate Swanson, former director for Iran at the National Security Council, about what President Trump understood about Iran before going to war.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Sen. Richard Blumenthal talks about Sen. Markwayne Mullin's DHS confirmation hearing
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks to Sen. Richard Blumenthal, D-Conn., who questioned Sen. Markwayne Mullin, President Trump's nominee to lead the Department of Homeland Security.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks to Sen. Richard Blumenthal, D-Conn., who questioned Sen. Markwayne Mullin, President Trump's nominee to lead the Department of Homeland Security.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Event organizers reckon with abuse allegations against activist Cesar Chavez
item.description: Organizers of events named for or honoring Cesar Chavez around the country are now cancelling or re-branding them in light of reports of the farm labor activist's past alleged abuses.
item.content:encoded: Organizers of events named for or honoring Cesar Chavez around the country are now cancelling or re-branding them in light of reports of the farm labor activist's past alleged abuses.
item.dc:creator: Michael Adkison

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Iran attacks world's largest liquified natural gas complex, Sen. Markwayne Mullin faces lawmakers at DHS confirmation hearing, organizers reckon with abuse allegations against activist Caesar Chavez.
item.content:encoded: Iran attacks world's largest liquified natural gas complex, Sen. Markwayne Mullin faces lawmakers at DHS confirmation hearing, organizers reckon with abuse allegations against activist Caesar Chavez.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Cillian Murphy on reprising his role as Tommy Shelby for 'Peaky Blinders' film
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with actor Cillian Murphy about his return to the role of gangster Tommy Shelby for the film "Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man."
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with actor Cillian Murphy about his return to the role of gangster Tommy Shelby for the film "Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man."
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Japan's PM to visit Trump as president continues pleas for help with Strait of Hormuz
item.description: As Japan's Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi prepares for her first White House visit, President Donald Trump calls for Japan and other allies to send warships to the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded: As Japan's Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi prepares for her first White House visit, President Donald Trump calls for Japan and other allies to send warships to the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kuhn

item.title: Voice memos hint at what life is like inside Iran during the war
item.description: Iran has imposed a near-total internet blackout as the war with the U.S. and Israel rages on. Some Iranians are sending voice memos to share a glimpse of what life is like inside the country.
item.content:encoded: Iran has imposed a near-total internet blackout as the war with the U.S. and Israel rages on. Some Iranians are sending voice memos to share a glimpse of what life is like inside the country.
item.dc:creator: Arezou Rezvani

item.title: Federal Reserve holds benchmark interest rate steady amid wartime uncertainty
item.description: The Federal Reserve held its benchmark interest rate steady this week. The central bank says it's too soon to know how the wartime spike in energy prices will affect overall inflation.
item.content:encoded: The Federal Reserve held its benchmark interest rate steady this week. The central bank says it's too soon to know how the wartime spike in energy prices will affect overall inflation.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: ICE officers are taking DNA samples from protesters they've arrested
item.description: People arrested while protesting ICE say federal agents took samples of their DNA. It's legal, but experts say the practice raises questions about what the government is doing with that genetic data.
item.content:encoded:
People arrested while protesting ICE say federal agents took samples of their DNA. It's legal, but experts say the practice raises questions about what the government is doing with that genetic data.

(Image credit: Stephen Maturen)
item.dc:creator: Meg Anderson
